title: Gynochthodes jasminoides
taxonomy: Gynochthodes jasminoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is commonly known as the jasmine vine or jasmine climber due to its fragrant flowers that resemble those of the jasmine plant. The plant is native to Southeast Asia and is often cultivated for its ornamental value. Gynochthodes jasminoides is classified in the genus Gynochthodes and is closely related to other species within the Rubiaceae family. The classification of Gynochthodes jasminoides is further specified as a member of the Rubus order.

The plant is said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses.
